$schema_note: Expected-outcomes manifest binding each synthetic fixture to the SPEC 13.2 T-id it exercises, the auth posture, the observable HTTP ack contract, and the ledger-side outcome (participant created / no-op / Exceptions reason). Drives BOTH scripts/verify-webhook-t1-t9.sh (which reads it to know what to assert) and tests/test_webhook.py (which asserts the manifest is internally coherent and the reasons are in the SPEC enum). All data is synthetic; zero PII, zero secrets, zero client identifiers.
registry_bound_location_note: The tenant the anthology registry is bound to for these fixtures. On the W5.3 canary, provision-anthology-client.sh binds the synthetic anthology to this location so T4/T5/T7 pass tenant_check and T6 (LOCwrongTenantZZZ) fails it.
valid_exception_reasons_note: The Exceptions.reason singleSelect enum (SPEC Section 7.3 EXCEPTIONS table). Any expect_exception_reason below MUST be one of these.
fingerprint_note: Idempotency key = sha256 over (contact_id, anthology_id, stage, payload body) (SPEC Section 5 PROCESS). t5-duplicate-intake.json is byte-identical to t4-valid-intake.json, so it carries the SAME fingerprint and must be a no-op acknowledge.

T1.probe: hooks mapping id 'anthology-intake' present in the gateway hooks config (openclaw config get hooks) OR an authenticated request reaches the transform (non-404)

T2.desc: requests without the route secret are refused
T2.note: Auth is enforced BEFORE route mapping (W0.4.json live probe: 401, not 404, on an unmapped path). intake_router.py ALSO refuses on its independent app-layer secret check (exit 2).

T3.desc: malformed payload lands in Exceptions with a reason, never a crash
T3.note: t3-malformed-notjson.txt is a NON-JSON body; the router must not crash on it. t3b-missing-ids.json is well-formed JSON but has no hidden ids. Both resolve to unroutable_missing_ids with the raw payload captured.

T4.desc: valid synthetic submission acknowledges in under 2 seconds and creates the participant

T5.desc: duplicate delivery of the same submission is a no-op acknowledge
T5.note: Same fingerprint as T4; exactly one participant and one S0 run must exist after both deliveries.

T8.desc: the REAL public URL through the named Cloudflare Tunnel accepts a submission end to end
T8.note: Deferred unless a real Cloudflare Tunnel public URL is supplied; NEVER Tailscale.

T9.desc: gateway restart preserves the route and the pending state (ledger intact, no lost event)
T9.note: Restarts the gateway, re-probes T1, and asserts the ledger cursor and any pending event survived.

credential_shaped_keys_forbidden_note: The legacy api_key payload field is ABOLISHED (SPEC Appendix B). No fixture may carry a credential-shaped key; the route secret rides the Authorization header at the transport, never the body. tests/test_webhook.py enforces this.
